AI_GUIDE_EXAMPLE.SUBTITLE: Discover step by step how IBIS-X analyzes your data and recommends the best ML approaches.
AI_GUIDE_EXAMPLE.STEP1_DESC: You upload a sales dataset with different columns: orders, customer types, products...
AI_GUIDE_EXAMPLE.STEP2_DESC: Our artificial intelligence examines your data and identifies analysis opportunities:
AI_GUIDE_EXAMPLE.CLASSIFICATION_REASON: AI detects that we can predict "Customer Type" (Premium/Standard) from other variables
AI_GUIDE_EXAMPLE.REGRESSION_REASON: AI sees that we can predict sales "Amount" from product/season characteristics
AI_GUIDE_EXAMPLE.STEP3_DESC: AI suggests the best algorithms with justifications:
AI_GUIDE_EXAMPLE.DECISION_TREE_WHY: Perfect to understand which criteria determine customer type. Highly explainable results.
AI_GUIDE_EXAMPLE.RANDOM_FOREST_WHY: More accurate if you prioritize performance over explainability.
AI_GUIDE_EXAMPLE.TAKEAWAY1: AI automatically understands the nature of your data
AI_GUIDE_EXAMPLE.TAKEAWAY2: Recommendations are tailored to your objectives

FAQ.TITLE: Everything You Need to Know
FAQ.SUBTITLE: Answers to the most common questions about IBIS-X and explainable artificial intelligence.

Q1.QUESTION: What is explainable artificial intelligence?
Q1.ANSWER: Explainable AI allows you to understand how and why a model makes its decisions. Unlike 'black boxes', our explanations clearly show you the factors that influence each prediction.

Q2.QUESTION: Can I use my own data?
Q2.ANSWER: Absolutely! IBIS X allows you to easily import your own datasets. Our system automatically adapts to your data and guides you through the analysis process.

Q3.QUESTION: How long does it take to get started?
Q3.ANSWER: You can launch your first analysis in under 15 seconds. Our intuitive interface and automated algorithms eliminate technical complexity.

Q4.QUESTION: What types of models are supported?
Q4.ANSWER: IBIS-X currently supports Decision Trees and Random Forests. These algorithms are particularly well-suited for explainability.

Q5.QUESTION: Is my data secure?
Q5.ANSWER: Yes, we comply with the strictest GDPR standards. Your data is encrypted and you maintain full control over its use and sharing.

Q6.QUESTION: Do I need technical knowledge?
Q6.ANSWER: No technical knowledge required! IBIS X was designed to make AI accessible to everyone, from beginners to experts.

FOOTER.TAGLINE: Explainable artificial intelligence within everyone's reach

DATASET_DETAIL.OVERALL_SCORE: Global quality score based on completeness, consistency, accuracy and ethical compliance of the data. The higher the score, the more reliable the dataset for your analyses.
DATASET_DETAIL.GLOBAL_SCORE_METRIC: Overall evaluation combining all quality criteria: completeness, consistency, accuracy and ethical compliance
DATASET_DETAIL.COMPLETENESS: Percentage of data present compared to expected data. High completeness means few missing values
DATASET_DETAIL.CONSISTENCY: Measures data coherence: uniform formats, absence of duplicates, respect for constraints. High consistency facilitates analysis
DATASET_DETAIL.ETHICAL_COMPLIANCE: Evaluates compliance with ethical principles: informed consent, anonymization, transparency, equity and personal data protection
DATASET_DETAIL.GENERAL_INFO_ICON: Metadata and technical characteristics of the dataset: source, accessibility, representativity and data structure
DATASET_DETAIL.QUALITY_METRICS_ICON: Automatic evaluation of dataset quality according to 4 criteria: completeness, consistency, ethical compliance and general reliability
DATASET_DETAIL.REPRESENTATIVITY: Indicates to what extent the data sample faithfully represents the target population. Good representativity ensures the validity of analyses.
DATASET_DETAIL.BALANCE: Measures the balance between different classes or categories in the dataset. Good balance avoids bias in ML models.
DATASET_DETAIL.SPLIT: Indicates if the dataset is already divided into training and test sets, facilitating ML model evaluation
DATASET_DETAIL.ANONYMIZATION: Confirms that personally identifiable data has been anonymized or pseudonymized to protect privacy
DATASET_DETAIL.TEMPORAL_FACTORS: Indicates if the dataset contains temporal variables important to consider in the analysis (dates, time series)
DATASET_DETAIL.METADATA: Confirms that detailed metadata accompanies the dataset (column descriptions, context, methodology)

NO_FILES.TITLE: No files available
NO_FILES.MESSAGE: This dataset does not contain accessible files for preview.

NO_PREVIEW.MESSAGE: Data preview is not available for this dataset.

QUALITY_ALERTS_MESSAGES.MISSING_DATA_RECOMMENDATION: Consider imputation techniques or filter columns with too many missing values
QUALITY_ALERTS_MESSAGES.OUTLIERS_RECOMMENDATION: Analyze these values to determine if they are legitimate or should be treated
QUALITY_ALERTS_MESSAGES.PII_RECOMMENDATION: Verify anonymization and GDPR compliance before use

CLASSIFICATION_TREE.TITLE: Classification with Decision Tree
CLASSIFICATION_TREE.DESCRIPTION: Your model uses a series of simple questions to classify data, like a logical questionnaire.

CLASSIFICATION_FOREST.TITLE: Classification with Random Forest
CLASSIFICATION_FOREST.DESCRIPTION: Your model combines {treeCount} trees that "vote" together for more robust predictions.

REGRESSION_TREE.TITLE: Regression with Decision Tree
REGRESSION_TREE.DESCRIPTION: Your model predicts numerical values by dividing the data space into homogeneous zones.

REGRESSION_FOREST.TITLE: Regression with Random Forest
REGRESSION_FOREST.DESCRIPTION: Your model averages predictions from {treeCount} trees for more stable estimation.

BASELINE.TITLE: Comparison with Reference Model
BASELINE.SUBTITLE: Evaluate your model's performance against a simple baseline
BASELINE.MAJORITY_DESC: Always predicts the most frequent class - reference baseline
BASELINE.MEDIAN_DESC: Always predicts the median value - reference baseline

ASSESSMENT.EXCELLENT_MESSAGE: Exceptional performance! Your {algorithm} {action} with remarkable precision.
ASSESSMENT.GOOD_MESSAGE: Good performance! Your model works well with some possible optimizations.
ASSESSMENT.CORRECT_MESSAGE: Acceptable but improvable performance. Consider hyperparameter optimization.
ASSESSMENT.TO_IMPROVE_MESSAGE: Poor performance. Check data quality or try {alternative}.

TIPS.CONFUSION_MATRIX: The confusion matrix shows where your model makes mistakes. The diagonal represents correct predictions. Frequent confusions indicate classes that are difficult to distinguish.
TIPS.ROC_CURVE: The ROC curve evaluates discrimination ability. The further it moves from the diagonal toward the upper left corner, the better the model.
TIPS.REGRESSION_ANALYSIS: The scatter plot compares predictions vs reality. Points should follow the ideal diagonal. The residuals histogram should be centered on zero.
TIPS.TREE_SIMPLE: The tree shows the decision logic. Follow the branches to understand how a prediction is made. The deeper the tree, the more it risks overfitting.
TIPS.TREE_FOREST: This tree is a representative sample of your forest of {treeCount} trees. Random Forest combines their decisions for more robustness.

TYPES.FEATURE_IMPORTANCE_DESC: Rank the influence of each variable

METHODS.AUTO_DESC: Automatic selection of the best method

MISSING_DATA.TITLE: Data Completeness Score
MISSING_DATA.SUBTITLE: Analysis of missing values in the dataset
MISSING_DATA.EXCLUDED_EXPLANATION: The following columns have been automatically excluded from the analysis as they correspond to technical identifiers:

SUGGESTIONS.MINIMAL_CLEANING_TOOLTIP: The low percentage of missing values allows for simple cleaning by deletion or basic imputation
SUGGESTIONS.IMPUTE_MEAN_TOOLTIP: For numerical data, replace missing values with the column mean
SUGGESTIONS.IMPUTE_MODE_TOOLTIP: For categorical data, replace missing values with the most frequent value
SUGGESTIONS.CAREFUL_ANALYSIS_TOOLTIP: The high rate of missing values requires analysis to understand the causes before treatment
SUGGESTIONS.CONSIDER_REMOVAL_TOOLTIP: The very high rate of missing values might justify removing this column

AI_GUIDE.TITLE: Personalized AI Guide
AI_GUIDE.SUBTITLE: Our artificial intelligence will analyze this dataset and recommend the best ML approach
AI_GUIDE.ANALYZING_SUBTITLE: AI is studying your dataset characteristics
AI_GUIDE.DATASET_ANALYSIS_SUBTITLE: What AI understands from your data

HERO.TITLE: Understand every decision your AI models make
HERO.SUBTITLE: Discover LIME and SHAP, the two pillars of modern explainability. Transform your black boxes into transparent and auditable decisions.
HERO.PREDICTION_DETAIL: This application was approved thanks to a favorable profile

METHODS.TITLE: The Two Methods of Explainability
METHODS.SUBTITLE: LIME and SHAP offer complementary approaches to understand your models. Discover their strengths and use cases.

USE_LIME.TITLE: Use LIME when...
USE_LIME.ITEM1: You need to explain a specific decision (medical diagnosis, credit approval)
USE_LIME.ITEM2: The decision directly impacts an individual and requires justification
USE_LIME.ITEM3: You want a simple and intuitive explanation for non-experts

USE_SHAP.TITLE: Use SHAP when...
USE_SHAP.ITEM1: You're analyzing your model's overall performance
USE_SHAP.ITEM2: You're optimizing the most important features
USE_SHAP.ITEM3: You're conducting research or in-depth scientific analysis

DEMO.TITLE: Interactive Demonstration
DEMO.SUBTITLE: Experiment with LIME and SHAP on a concrete loan approval decision example.

BENEFITS.TITLE: Why Explainability is Essential
BENEFITS.SUBTITLE: XAI transforms your models into trusted partners for decision-making.

VIDEOS.TITLE: Learn with our Video Tutorials
VIDEOS.SUBTITLE: Visual and practical explanations to master LIME and SHAP quickly.

CTA.TITLE: Ready to make your models explainable?
CTA.SUBTITLE: Start your first XAI analysis with IBIS-X right now

OBJECTIVE_STEP_ENHANCED.TITLE: Task & Target Variable Selection
OBJECTIVE_STEP_ENHANCED.SUBTITLE: Define your learning objective with artificial intelligence

TARGET_SELECTION.TITLE: 🎯 Target Variable to Predict
TARGET_SELECTION.DESCRIPTION: Choose the column you want the AI to learn to predict

TASK_SUGGESTION.TITLE: 🧠 AI Automatic Suggestion
TASK_SUGGESTION.DESCRIPTION: Our AI analyzes your data and recommends the optimal learning task
TASK_SUGGESTION.OVERRIDE_WARNING: You can change this suggestion if you prefer a different task type

TASK_TYPE_SELECTION.TITLE: 📋 Learning Task Type
TASK_TYPE_SELECTION.DESCRIPTION: Select the type of problem you want to solve
TASK_TYPE_SELECTION.CLASSIFICATION_DESC: Predict categories or classes (e.g., spam/not-spam, customer type)
TASK_TYPE_SELECTION.REGRESSION_DESC: Predict continuous numeric values (e.g., price, temperature, age)

BINNING_OPTIONS.TITLE: ⚙️ Binning Configuration
BINNING_OPTIONS.DESCRIPTION: Transform your numeric variable into classes for classification
BINNING_OPTIONS.QUANTILES_METHOD: Quantiles (same number of samples per class)
BINNING_OPTIONS.WARNING: Binning creates artificial classes—prefer regression if you can

TARGET_LEAKAGE.TITLE: 🛡️ Target Leakage Protection
TARGET_LEAKAGE.DESCRIPTION: We automatically exclude the target column from features to prevent data leakage
TARGET_LEAKAGE.EXCLUDED_COUNT: {{count}} column(s) excluded to prevent target leakage

WARNINGS.NUMERIC_AS_CLASSIFICATION: Numeric variable used for classification - consider regression
WARNINGS.CATEGORICAL_AS_REGRESSION: Categorical variable used for regression - consider classification
WARNINGS.HIGH_CARDINALITY: Variable with many unique values - performance potentially affected

WEBSHAP.TITLE: WebSHAP Explainability
WEBSHAP.SUBTITLE: Interactive SHAP values in real-time
WEBSHAP.NO_DATA: No data available for SHAP explanations

ALGORITHM.TITLE: WebSHAP Algorithm
ALGORITHM.DESCRIPTION: WebSHAP uses the Kernel SHAP algorithm to compute feature importance directly in the browser

INTERPRETATION.TITLE: Results interpretation
INTERPRETATION.DESCRIPTION: Each bar represents the contribution of a feature to the final prediction
INTERPRETATION.BAR_LENGTH: Bar length represents the magnitude of contribution
